title: Identity, Permissions, Ownership, umask, and ACLs · Terminal Checkpoint
summary: Create a shared operations directory that grants required access without world-writable shortcuts or unexplained privilege escalation. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence.
Calculate read, write, and execute effects for owner, group, and other on regular files and directories.
Translate symbolic and octal chmod operations while preserving unrelated bits and verifying the result.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the User Group Identity evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps User Group Identity assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs. Competency probe: Interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so User Group Identity has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the User Group Identity precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs. Competency probe: Interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A username alone determines every permission decision.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior for the quiz User Group Identity case
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary for the quiz User Group Identity case
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output for the quiz User Group Identity case
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff for the quiz User Group Identity case

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Special Bits Acls: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs. Competency probe: Use setgid directories, sticky bit, setuid risk, and POSIX ACL masks only when the base ownership model is insufficient.
steps.why: Retrieving Special Bits Acls through the concrete a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use setgid directories, sticky bit, setuid risk, and POSIX ACL masks only when the base ownership model is insufficient.

hints: Name the Special Bits Acls precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
